prompt:
Data platform team here. Our nightly account loads into Salesforce still go through a contractor's SOAP-era jar that nobody can rebuild, and it silently drops the failure report. I want a small stdlib-only Python package, sf_bulk, that runs a full Bulk API 2.0 ingest end to end so our scheduler can import it. No pip installs, no simple_salesforce.

Step zero, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 developer guide on developer.salesforce.com — at minimum the create-job, upload-job-data, close-job, job-info, failed-results and unprocessed-records pages, plus the CSV preparation rules. Confirm the current stable API version, the exact resource paths, the create/close JSON bodies, the CSV content type and line-ending contract, the documented job state machine, and the failed/unprocessed result CSV shapes. Our pinned understanding of all of that lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both are protected fixtures, as is the test file. Compare the live official docs against the fixture; if they disagree, call that out explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. In your final message cite the exact official URLs you actually fetched and read.

What I need in package sf_bulk (client.py, csvio.py, ingest.py, errors.py plus __init__.py — split further if you like):

- sf_bulk.client.BulkClient(instance_url, access_token, api_version="v67.0"): create_ingest_job(sobject, operation, line_ending="LF"), upload_job_data(job, fieldnames, rows), close_job(job_id), abort_job(job_id), get_job_info(job_id), get_failed_results(job_id), get_unprocessed_records(job_id). Bearer auth on every request, JSON accept/content types where the docs say so, and the data upload exactly as documented (right method, the job's contentUrl used verbatim — careful, Salesforce returns it without a leading slash — and the right content type). Result CSVs come back parsed into lists of dicts.
- CSV building in sf_bulk.csvio: UTF-8, header row from fieldnames, the job's declared line ending terminating every record including the last, None rendered the way the CSV prep docs say Salesforce expects nulls, and proper double-quote escaping for values holding delimiters, quotes or newlines. The tests compare uploaded bytes exactly.
- sf_bulk.ingest.run_ingest(client, sobject, fieldnames, rows, operation="insert", line_ending="LF", poll_interval=2.0, max_polls=20, sleep=time.sleep) -> IngestReport with job_id, state, records_processed, records_failed, error_message, failed, unprocessed. It creates ONE job, uploads, closes, then polls job info with the injected sleep between polls (never time.sleep directly) until a documented terminal state; more than max_polls polls raises sf_bulk.errors.PollTimeout. After a terminal state, download the failed-results CSV when the job Failed or reported failed records, and the unprocessed-records CSV when it Failed or was Aborted — and only then.
- The part that actually bites us in production: the close call is a state change, not idempotent. If the connection dies mid-close (we get no status line back), the job may or may not be closed. On a transport-level failure during close, run_ingest must re-read the job info and decide: still Open means send the close again; anything past Open means it landed and the PATCH must NOT be repeated (Salesforce answers INVALIDJOBSTATE if you do). Never create a second job while recovering. Wrap connection-level failures in sf_bulk.errors.TransportError.
- Salesforce API errors arrive as a JSON array of errorCode/message objects; raise sf_bulk.errors.BulkApiError carrying status_code, error_code, message. The access token must never appear in exception text, and never follow a redirect to a different origin with our Authorization header attached — refuse or strip, your call.

test_bulk_ingest.py is the acceptance contract; it spins up a loopback fake org, so no real Salesforce and no real credentials are ever involved. Run python3 test_bulk_ingest.py until green. Don't touch the test file or anything under docs/.

research.interface: Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 ingest REST resources under /services/data/v67.0/jobs/ingest (Summer '26, API version 67.0)

official_sources.title: Create a Job | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: POST /services/data/vXX.X/jobs/ingest with Authorization: Bearer; request fields object (required), operation (insert/delete/hardDelete/update/upsert), contentType (optional, default CSV), lineEnding (optional, default LF), externalIdFieldName (upsert only); response fields id, state, contentUrl, apiVersion; JobStateEnum Open/UploadComplete/Aborted/JobComplete/Failed

official_sources.title: Upload Job Data | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: PUT to the job's contentUrl (/services/data/vXX.X/jobs/ingest/jobID/batches) with Content-Type: text/csv and the raw CSV body; success status 201 (Created); guidance to keep local CSV data until processing results are confirmed

official_sources.title: Close or Abort a Job | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: PATCH /services/data/vXX.X/jobs/ingest/jobID with JSON body {"state": "UploadComplete"} (or "Aborted"); required for every ingest job — without it processing never starts; response returns the job metadata record

official_sources.title: Get Information About an Ingest Job | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: GET /services/data/vXX.X/jobs/ingest/jobID; full JobStateEnum with meanings (Open, UploadComplete, InProgress, JobComplete, Aborted, Failed); numberRecordsProcessed, numberRecordsFailed, errorMessage; contentUrl only valid while Open

official_sources.title: Get Job Failed Record Results | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: GET /services/data/vXX.X/jobs/ingest/jobID/failedResults/ returns a CSV with sf__Error and sf__Id columns followed by the original record columns; row order not guaranteed

official_sources.title: Get Job Unprocessed Record Results | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: GET /services/data/vXX.X/jobs/ingest/jobID/unprocessedrecords/ returns a CSV of records the job never processed (failed or aborted jobs); unprocessed rows are distinct from failed rows

official_sources.title: Prepare CSV Files | Bulk API 2.0 and Bulk API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: CSV files must be UTF-8; supported line endings are LF and CRLF with LF the default, matched to the job's lineEnding field; nulls are written as #N/A; double quotes escape embedded delimiters/quotes/line endings; field values are never trimmed

research.language_rationale: Salesforce ships no official Python SDK for Bulk API 2.0; the vendor-documented integration surface is plain REST plus CSV bodies, and Python's stdlib urllib/csv modules are the idiomatic zero-dependency way to run scheduled bulk loads from a data-engineering job.
